title: HUD, Text, Menus, Focus, Multichannel Cues, and Motion Settings · Program Brief: Readable Text
summary: Tiny centered text scales poorly, focus is color-only, menus require a mouse, damage flashes rapidly, status disappears immediately, and reduced motion removes essential information. Predict player-visible behavior, state, units, timing, input, rendering, feedback, failure, accessibility, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the readable text increment in accessible responsive game interface and settings flow. Learners progress through prediction, e…
objectives: Choose scalable fonts, line spacing, contrast, alignment, safe areas, localization space, and structured labels for essential text.
Continue play with missing files, unavailable mixer, device change, interruption, or mute while preserving diagnostics.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: asteroids-readable-text — Choose scalable fonts, line spacing, contrast, alignment, safe areas, localization space, and structured labels for essential text.
# Evidence: asteroids-worked-asteroids-hud-menus-accessibility-4
# Competency: asteroids-worked-asteroids-hud-menus-accessibility-4.
# Case 1655: a low-vision laptop playtest at 125 percent scaling.
# Operating constraint: test empty, zero-vector, seam, low-frame-rate, focus-loss, device-loss, missing-asset, corrupted-save, and rejected cases.
# Changed case: inject a 400 millisecond frame stall and prove the update budget prevents a spiral of death.
def worked_asteroids_hud_menus_accessibility_4(critical_signals: int = 8, multi_channel_signals: int = 8, focusable_controls: int = 7, focused_controls: int = 7, reduced_motion: bool = True, scalable_text: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "playerworked4",
        "seed1655",
        "input4dekrow",
        "frameworked41655",
        "state1655worked4",
        "playtest4dekrow1655",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    if critical_signals < 1 or focusable_controls < 1:
        return False, critical_signals, "ui-domain"
    if multi_channel_signals != critical_signals:
        return False, multi_channel_signals, "color-only-signal"
    if focused_controls != focusable_controls:
        return False, focused_controls, "focus-gap"
    if not reduced_motion or not scalable_text:
        return False, focusable_controls, "display-access"
    assert focused_controls == focusable_controls
    return True, focused_controls, "accessible-game-ui"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Build Readable Text evidence · Theory Readable Text
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: asteroids-readable-text-brtui2" that uses case 6417 to build readable keyboard and controller menus, HUD, focus, status, multi-channel cues, text scaling, and reduced-motion options. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native pygame-ce, SDL, window, controller, mixer, filesystem, package, GPU, installer, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, released pygame-ce 2.5.7, fixed and headless SDL fixtures, real devices, licensed assets, accessibility playtests, profiling, clean installations, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-readable-text-brtui2
# Competency: asteroids-readable-text.
# Case 6417: an offline cross-platform release rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: retain player goal, revision, Python, pygame-ce, SDL, fixture, seed, input trace, viewport, and result identity.
# Changed case: lose window focus while thrust and fire are held, then prove both actions release safely.
def evidence_asteroids_readable_text_brtui2(critical_signals: int = 8, multi_channel_signals: int = 8, focusable_controls: int = 7, focused_controls: int = 7, reduced_motion: bool = True, scalable_text: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_brtui2 = "brtui2-2iutrb-6417"
    assert evidence_variant_brtui2
    evidence_axes_brtui2 = (
        "playerbrtui2",
        "seed6417",
        "input2iutrb",
        "framebrtui26417",
        "state6417brtui2",
        "playtest2iutrb6417",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_brtui2)) == 6
    if critical_signals < 1 or focusable_controls < 1:
        return False, critical_signals, "ui-domain"
    if multi_channel_signals != critical_signals:
        return False, multi_channel_signals, "color-only-signal"
    if focused_controls != focusable_controls:
        return False, focused_controls, "focus-gap"
    if not reduced_motion or not scalable_text:
        return False, focusable_controls, "display-access"
    assert focused_controls == focusable_controls
    return True, focused_controls, "accessible-game-ui"

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Readable Text structural check is only one gate. accessible responsive game interface and settings flow must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

hints: For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Build Readable Text evidence · Theory Readable Text, name the player, revision, runtime, seed, input, time, coordinate, state, entity, collision, render, cue, accessibility, package, release, and desktop-transfer boundary, Readable Text precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Build Readable Text evidence · Theory Readable Text, trace this concrete input and predict event, intent, simulation, collision, cleanup, rendering, cue, accessibility, failure, pause, rollback, recovery, and desktop evidence before reviewing it: A theory Asteroids team handles case 5235 during an offline cross-platform release rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to build readable keyboard and controller menus, HUD, focus, status, multi-channel cues, text scaling, and reduced-motion options; keep every critical cue available through more than color or sound and every action remappable without timing precision; then replay the same input trace at 30, 60, and 144 presentation frames per second and compare simulation state. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Choose scalable fonts, line spacing, contrast, alignment, safe areas, localization space, and structured labels for essential text.

For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Build Readable Text evidence · Theory Readable Text, challenge the documented misconception with changed-frame-rate, stall, seam, input, device, collision, cue, accessibility, save, package, failure, recovery, and controlled desktop-transfer evidence: High resolution makes any font size readable.

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Readable Text model · Theory Readable Text
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Readable Text: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory Asteroids team handles case 3616 during a keyboard-only community arcade night. Build deterministic Python evidence to build readable keyboard and controller menus, HUD, focus, status, multi-channel cues, text scaling, and reduced-motion options; test empty, zero-vector, seam, low-frame-rate, focus-loss, device-loss, missing-asset, corrupted-save, and rejected cases; then cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Choose scalable fonts, line spacing, contrast, alignment, safe areas, localization space, and structured labels for essential text.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Readable Text model · Theory Readable Text.
steps.why: Retrieving Readable Text through the concrete accessible responsive game interface and settings flow case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Readable Text model · Theory Readable Text, keep Readable Text connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Choose scalable fonts, line spacing, contrast, alignment, safe areas, localization space, and structured labels for essential text.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.

checks.description: The cumulative python portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker # Evidence: asteroids-readable-text-brtui2.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: asteroids-readable-text-brtui2(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_asteroids_readable_text_brtui2\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?multi_channel_signals != critical_signals)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?focused_controls != focusable_controls)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not reduced_motion)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not scalable_text)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
checks.description: The increment includes the required executable or reviewable evidence structure.
checks.failureMessage: Complete the full function, prompt contract, or gate record shown in the brief.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
